title: Chloe Pei-Hsin Huang’s Research Blog
text: I am a 1st year PhD student in Psychology at Durham University. My research focuses on relationships, masking and wellbeing in autistic adults.

title: Chloe Pei-Hsin Huang’s Research Blog
text:
This website
This site is a space where I:

document my learning
publish bite-size blog for co-researchers (and with the wider audience)

title: BiB (Bite-size Blogs)
text:
Welcome to BiB! I summarise accessible information/theory/framework here, primarily with my co-researchers (i.e., Emma Whittaker, Iris Varela, Kieran Rose and Rose Matthews), but also targeting to a wider audience outside the academia.

River of Life









Jul 14, 2026

title: River of Life
text: River of Life is a tool that visualises individual’s experience with a ‘river’ metaphor. Participants are encouraged to draw a river with these key elements – river of life (key stages), tributaries (positive experiences), and rough waters (difficult challenges). After drawing the river, participants will be asked to share their own river with others.

title: River of Life
text:
Material

Multicoloured paper
Coloured markers, crayons, and pen
flipchart (optional)
Magazines, scissors, glue (optional)

title: River of Life
text:
Steps
 Figure made based on Moussa (2009), Howard (2023) and Carmody (2023)

title: River of Life
section: What we might need to think about & next steps:
text:
What we might need to think about & next steps:

Whether it should be held as focus groups or individual interviews
Visualise and simplify prompts and steps

title: River of Life
text:
References
Carmody, S. (2023). River of life storytelling. In Qualitative Research – a practical guide for health and social care researchers and practitioners (pp. 128–134). Monash University.
Howard, J. (2023). Practical Guides for Participatory Methods: Rivers of Life. The Institute of Development Studies and Partner Organisations.https://hdl.handle.net/20.500.12413/17839
Moussa. (2009). Rivers of life. In Community-based adaptation to climate change.
